chapter_title: About the Inviolability of the Muslims' Honors

hadith_entries.text:
146. Narrated Abu-Musa : 'The Prophet said: "A
faithful believer to a faithful believer is like the bricks of a
wall, reinforcing each other. While (saying that) the
Prophet clasped his hands, by interlocking his fingers.""
(Bukhari)

147. Narrated Abdullah ibn Omar: 'Allah's Messenger
said: "A Muslim is the brother of another Muslim, so he
should not oppress him nor should he hand him over to an
oppressor. Whoever fulfills the needs of his brother, Allah
will fulfill his needs; whoever brings his (Muslim) brother
out of discomfort, Allah will bring him out of the
discomforts of the Day of Resurrection, and whoever veils.
the defects of a Muslim, Allah will screen his defects on
the Day of Resurrection." (Bukhari)
رَوَاهُ مُسْلِمٌ.
Riyadhos-Saliheen (The Meadows of the Righteous)

148. Narrated Abu-Huraira: 'Allah's Messenger
said: "A Muslim has six duties towards another Muslim:
when you meet him greet him, when he invites you accept
his invitation, when he asks your advice give it to him,
when he sneezes and praises Allah say to him: 'May Allah
have mercy on you,' when he is ill visit him, and when he
dies follow his funeral procession." (Muslim)
*
-
